a: An object that describes the user’s request to add media items to their library or to a specific playlist.
The service’s response to a request to confirm an add media intent.
The result of receiving the user’s confirmation that they want to add the media items to the destination.
Your service’s response to a request to handle a fully resolved add media intent.
The result of handling an intent to add media items to a specified library or playlist.
A request to process an add media intent.
The parameters of an add media intent request.
The service’s response to a request to process an add media intent.
Your service’s response to a request to resolve media items in an add media intent.
The result of attempting to modify the user’s library or a playlist.
Your service’s response to a request to resolve media items in an update media affinity intent.
The result of attempting to identify the media items to add to the user’s library or to a playlist.
A structure that contains a response code indicating your service’s progress in handling an add media intent.
Codes your service can return when confirming or handling an add media intent.
The user’s library or a specified playlist, or information about why your service can’t use the requested destination.
A result that requires the user to confirm the library or playlist before you add media items to it.
A result that requires the user to choose which library or playlist they want to add media items to.
A media destination that successfully matches an intent.
The reason your service can’t add media items to the specified library or playlist.
Reasons the media service can’t add media items to a specified playlist.
A media item that matches an add media intent, or information about why your service can’t provide a media item.
A result that requires the user to confirm the media item before you add it to their library or to a playlist.
A result that requires the user to choose which media item they want to add to their library or to a playlist.
A media item that successfully matches the intent.
The reason your service can’t add the media item to the user’s library or to a playlist.
Reasons the media service can’t add the media item to the user’s library or to a playlist.
A Boolean value that matches an intent parameter, or information about why your service can’t determine the value.
A result that requires the user to confirm the Boolean value before proceeding.
A Boolean value that successfully matches the intent.
Provide configuration details for your media server’s endpoints to a HomePod speaker or an Apple TV.
Client-originated limitations on how to process a request, such as including explicit content and how much content the client device can receive in a response.
A description of a piece of playback content, such as a song, podcast, or advertisement.
Metadata for some media content.
Imagery for media content, such as an album cover.
An object that describes why the client can’t play a specific piece of content.
An identifier for a song, podcast, ad, or other media content. The identifier must be stable and unique within a queue.
A request the client sends to recover from failed content playback.
A response that allows the client to recover from failed content playback.
A request for an item’s content protection key.
A response to a request for an item’s content protection key.
The content protection key systems that SiriKit Cloud Media supports.
A full or partial date and time.
A period of time from a specified start date to a specified end date.
Timing information to isolate service performance from network delays.
A date or time in specified of units (such as year, month, day, hour, and minute) for evaluation in a calendar system and time zone.
Instructions for accessing your media service’s endpoints.
Headers to include with all requests to the media service.
Instructions for accessing your media service’s intent endpoints.
Configuration details for your service’s add media intent.
Headers to include with requests to intent endpoints.
Configuration details for your service’s play media intent.
Configuration details for your service’s update media affinity intent.
Instructions for accessing your service’s media endpoints.
Configuration details for your service’s media queue.
Configuration details for your service’s content playback failure endpoint.
Configuration details for your service’s content protection key endpoint.
Configuration details for your service’s content protection system.
Headers to include with requests to the content protection key endpoint.
Headers to include with requests to media endpoints.
Configuration details for your service’s media playback queue.
Headers to include with requests to media endpoints.
Configuration details for your service’s update activity endpoint.
Headers to include with requests to the update activity endpoint.
A unique identifier for a specific media service configuration.
Instructions for accessing an intent endpoint.
Headers to include with requests to intent endpoints.
Provide a playback queue from a successfully processed play media intent.
A user request for your service to fulfill.
An object that matches a parameter of an intent, or information about why your service can’t determine a value for the parameter.
An empty object that indicates the service must have a value for this parameter, but the intent doesn’t include one.
An empty object that indicates the intent doesn’t include a value for this parameter, but the server can proceed without one.
An empty object that indicates the server doesn’t support this parameter.
Your service’s response to an intent.
Properties that clients include in requests to all intent endpoints.
The parameters of a client’s request.
Properties to include in responses from all intent endpoints.
The outcome of handling an intent.
A preference or dislike for a media item.
A media affinity that matches an update media affinity intent, or information about why your service can’t determine the media affinity.
A result that requires the user to confirm the media affinity before proceeding.
A media affinity that successfully matches the intent.
The user’s library or a playlist.
The user’s library as a destination for an add media intent.
A playlist as a destination for an add media intent.
A particular piece of media that an intent references, such as a song, podcast episode, or playlist.
Types of media items or media searches.
A way of identifying the current media item rather than with metadata.
A description of the media items the user wants to play, add to a playlist, or express a preference for.
A prioritization for search results.
A configuration for permitted user interactions and other player behaviors during playback.
Options for reporting playback progress.
A set of modifications to apply to the default set of available playback controls.
Default playback controls and settings for common content types.
An object that describes the user’s request to play a media item.
Your service’s response to a request to handle a fully resolved play media intent.
The result of handling an intent to play a media item.
A request to process a play media intent.
The parameters of a play media intent request.
The service’s response to a request to process a play media intent.
Your service’s response to a request to resolve media items in a play media intent.
The results of resolving the media items in an intent to play media.
Your service’s response to a request to resolve whether a play media intent requests a shuffled queue.
The result of resolving whether a play media intent shuffles the playback queue.
Your service’s response to a request to resolve the queue location in a play media intent.
The result of resolving the queue location for a play media intent.
Your service’s response to a request to resolve the repeat mode in a play media intent.
The result of resolving the repeat mode for a play media intent.
Your service’s response to a request to resolve whether a play media intent resumes the current playback queue.
The result of resolving whether a play media intent resumes the current playback queue.
A structure that contains a response code indicating how your service handles a play media intent.
Codes your service can return when handling a play media intent.
A media item that matches a play media intent, or information about why your service can’t provide a media item.
A result that requires the user to confirm the media item before proceeding.
A result that requires the user to choose from multiple media items before proceeding.
A media item that successfully matches the intent.
The reason your service can’t play the requested media item.
Reasons the media service can’t play the media item.
A request for a media playback queue.
Possible locations in a playback queue to insert a media item.
Information about whether your service can resolve the specified queue location.
A result that requires the user to confirm the playback mode before proceeding.
A queue location that matches the intent.
The possible repeat modes for a media queue.
Information about whether your service can identify the specified repeat mode.
A result that requires the user to confirm the playback mode before proceeding.
A playback mode that successfully matches the intent.
Information about the current playback content.
Interpret the user’s request to play a media item, and provide instructions to access a corresponding playback queue.
Add media items to the user’s library or to a playlist.

t: Process an Update Media Affinity Intent
a: Record the user’s preference for a specific media item or a broader category of media.
An exception response from a media service.
A response object that indicates when the service fails to process the client’s request.
Categories of exceptions a service can encounter.
A sequence of media content for playback, with links to the previous and next segments of a full playback queue.
An event that occurs during content playback.
A dictionary of configuration names and the media controls they permit.
A stable identifier for a playback queue.
Instructions for editing the current playback queue.
A position within a playback queue.
Provide a recovery queue that allows the client to resume playback after an error.
Monitor progress through the playback queue.

t: Retrieve an Asset’s Content Protection Key
a: Provide the content key for a specific protected asset.
A URI that references debugging information for a request.
Information the client provides about a sequence of requests and responses to process an intent.
Stream music directly to HomePod speakers from your media service.
An object that describes a system framework error.
A report of the client’s current playback state and recent user interaction, and an opportunity for your service to modify the client’s playback queue.
Updates to the client’s queue and user activity in response to a report of playback progress.
An object that describes a user’s stated preference regarding media items.
Your service’s response to a request to handle a fully resolved update media affinity intent.
The result of handling an intent to update media affinity.
A request to process an update media affinity intent.
The parameters of an update media affinity intent request.
The service’s response to a request to process an update media affinity intent.
Your service’s response to a request that expresses a preference or dislike for a media item.
The results of resolving the media affinity in an update media affinity intent.
Your service’s response to a request to resolve media items in an update media affinity intent.
The results of resolving the media items in an intent to update media affinity.
A structure that contains a response code indicating how your service handles an update media affinity intent.
Codes your service can return when handling an update media affinity intent.
A media item that matches an update media affinity intent, or information about why your service can’t provide a media item.
A result that requires the user to confirm the media item before proceeding.
A result that requires the user to choose from multiple media items before proceeding.
A media item that successfully matches the intent.
The reason your service can’t update information about the requested media item.
Reasons the media service can’t update information about the media item.
The context for playing a media queue.
A dictionary that contains service-specific state information necessary to continue an activity in a separate request.
